note_data.title: Poetry — Part I
keyPoints: Poetry is the expression of feelings and ideas in verse form with rhythm, meter and often rhyme
A stanza is a group of lines forming a unit in a poem, separated by spaces from other stanzas
Rhyme scheme is the pattern of rhymes at the end of each line, denoted by letters (ABAB, AABB)
Meter is the rhythmic structure of verse, based on stressed and unstressed syllables
Alliteration is the repetition of initial consonant sounds in consecutive words
Metaphor is a direct comparison between two unlike things without using 'like' or 'as'
Simile is a comparison between two unlike things using 'like' or 'as'
Personification gives human qualities to non-human things
The theme is the central message or main idea of the poem

definitions.definition: Poetry is the art of rhythmical composition, written or spoken, for exciting pleasure by beautiful, imaginative or elevated thoughts
A stanza is a division of a poem consisting of a series of lines arranged together in a usually recurring pattern of meter and rhyme
Rhyme is the repetition of similar sounds in two or more words, most often at the end of lines in poems and songs
Alliteration is the occurrence of the same letter or sound at the beginning of adjacent or closely connected words
A metaphor is a figure of speech that makes an implicit, implied or hidden comparison between two things that are unrelated

importantQuestions.question: Define poetry and mention its two main characteristics.
importantQuestions.answer: Poetry is the art of rhythmical composition for exciting pleasure by beautiful thoughts. Its main characteristics are: (1) Rhythmic pattern with meter (2) Expression of emotions and feelings in verse form.

importantQuestions.question: What are the different figures of speech used in poetry? Explain with examples.
importantQuestions.answer: The main figures of speech in poetry are: (1) Simile - comparison using 'like' or 'as' (e.g., brave as a lion) (2) Metaphor - direct comparison without 'like/as' (e.g., life is a journey) (3) Personification - giving human qualities to non-human things (e.g., the wind whispered) (4) Alliteration - repetition of initial sounds (e.g., Peter Piper picked) (5) Hyperbole - deliberate exaggeration for effect. These devices make poetry more expressive and beautiful.

mcqs.question: Which of the following is an example of alliteration?
A group of lines in a poem is called:
'The wind whispered through the trees' is an example of:

commonMistakes: Students confuse simile with metaphor - remember simile uses 'like' or 'as'
Writing rhyme scheme incorrectly - use capital letters ABAB, not lowercase
Confusing stanza with verse - stanza is a group of lines, verse is the entire poem
Not giving proper examples when explaining figures of speech
Forgetting to mention rhythm and meter as essential elements of poetry
examTips: This chapter usually has 2-3 MCQs on figures of speech in every BSEK paper
Always give examples when defining figures of speech - marks are awarded for examples
Questions on rhyme scheme identification appear frequently - practice with sample poems
Long questions often ask to identify and explain figures of speech from given verses
note_data.keyDiagram: Rhyme Scheme Pattern Diagram - showing how to mark ABAB, AABB, ABCB patterns in poem stanzas with connecting lines between rhyming words
pastPaperRefs: BSEK 2023 SSC-II Q3 (4 marks) — asked students to define poetry and identify two main types with examples
BSEK 2022 SSC-II Q2 (3 marks) — required definition of lyric poetry and one example from textbook
BSEK 2021 SSC-II Q4 (5 marks) — asked to differentiate between narrative and dramatic poetry with characteristics
BSEK 2020 SSC-II Q1 (3 marks) — definition of epic poetry and mention of famous epic from literature

workedExample.question: Define poetry and explain the difference between lyric and narrative poetry. (5 marks)
steps: Step 1: Write exact textbook definition of poetry
Step 2: Define lyric poetry using STBB textbook wording
Step 3: Define narrative poetry using STBB textbook wording
Step 4: State key difference between the two types
Step 5: Provide one example of each type
workedExample.answer: Poetry is the art of expressing thoughts, feelings and ideas in verse form with rhythmic and melodious language. Lyric poetry expresses personal emotions and feelings of the poet in a musical manner. Narrative poetry tells a story or relates a sequence of events through verse. The main difference is that lyric poetry focuses on emotions while narrative poetry focuses on storytelling. Example of lyric: 'The Daffodils' by Wordsworth. Example of narrative: 'The Ancient Mariner' by Coleridge.
workedExample.markScheme: 1 mark for poetry definition, 1 mark each for lyric and narrative definitions, 1 mark for difference, 1 mark for examples

examinerWarnings: Students often lose marks by writing general answers instead of textbook-exact definitions. Always use the STBB textbook phrasing for poetry definitions.
Many students confuse lyric poetry with song lyrics. Remember that lyric poetry specifically refers to personal emotional expression in verse form.
Avoid giving modern examples of poetry types. Stick to classical examples mentioned in the STBB textbook like Wordsworth, Coleridge, and Shakespeare.
note_data.quickRecap: Poetry is artistic expression in verse form with rhythm and melody. Main types: Lyric (personal emotions), Narrative (tells story), Dramatic (characters speak), and Epic (heroic deeds). Always use exact STBB textbook definitions in exams. Focus on classical examples from prescribed textbook.
